descriptions.bench: redis-benchmark driving one Redis instance directly, swept toward saturation. Default config: 50 parallel clients, 3-byte payload, no pipelining. (The client speaks HTTP in the catalog; here it stands for the RESP-speaking benchmark client — the numeric capacity path is protocol-agnostic.)
descriptions.redis: One single-threaded Redis instance modeled as an M/M/1 station: 1 connection slot, service time = connectionHeldMs (the per-command time on the single-threaded command loop), so capacity = 1000 / connectionHeldMs op/s (graph-read.ts finite-pool station). connectionHeldMs is the fitted tunable, shared across the SET and LPUSH entries; its catalog default (0.01 ms) is an ESTIMATED 100,000 op/s ceiling.
